description: Separate the opentabs CLI into two packages: @opentabs-dev/cli (user-facing: start, status, doctor, plugin management) and @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools (plugin developer: build). Remove dev/reload commands from the user CLI. Add opentabs start command. Update scaffold to reference plugin-tools instead of cli. Update E2E tests and publish script.

userStories.title: Create @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools package
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want a dedicated plugin-tools package so that my devDependencies only include what I need for building plugins.
acceptanceCriteria: platform/plugin-tools/ directory exists with package.json, tsconfig.json
package.json has name @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools, bin entry for opentabs-plugin
platform/plugin-tools/src/cli.ts is the entry point with shebang #!/usr/bin/env bun
opentabs-plugin build command works (same behavior as current opentabs build)
opentabs-plugin build --watch works
Source files for build command are moved from platform/cli/src/commands/build.ts to platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts
tsconfig.json has correct project references to shared and plugin-sdk
Root tsconfig.json includes platform/plugin-tools references
Root package.json workspaces pattern platform/* covers the new package
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Create platform/plugin-tools/ directory. Copy platform/cli/src/commands/build.ts to platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts. Create a minimal cli.ts entry point that registers just the build command. The package.json should depend on @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk, @opentabs-dev/shared, commander, picocolors, zod (same deps as the build command uses). The tsconfig.json should reference platform/shared and platform/plugin-sdk. Add references in root tsconfig.json for platform/plugin-tools and platform/plugin-tools/tsconfig.test.json. The workspace pattern platform/* already covers it. The bin entry should be: { "opentabs-plugin": "./dist/cli.js" }.

userStories.title: Remove dev, build, and reload commands from user CLI
userStories.description: As a user, I want the opentabs CLI to only show commands relevant to me so that the help output is clean and focused.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs build command is removed from @opentabs-dev/cli
opentabs dev command is removed from @opentabs-dev/cli
opentabs reload command is removed from @opentabs-dev/cli
platform/cli/src/commands/build.ts is deleted (moved to plugin-tools in US-001)
platform/cli/src/commands/dev.ts is deleted
platform/cli/src/commands/reload.ts is deleted
platform/cli/src/commands/index.ts no longer exports registerBuildCommand, registerDevCommand, registerReloadCommand
platform/cli/src/cli.ts no longer registers build, dev, or reload commands
@opentabs-dev/cli package.json dependencies no longer include @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk or zod (only needed for build)
opentabs --help shows only: start, status, doctor, setup, logs, plugin, config
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/cli/src/cli.ts to remove the three command registrations. Delete the three command files. Edit platform/cli/src/commands/index.ts to remove the three exports. Update platform/cli/package.json dependencies — the cli no longer needs plugin-sdk or zod (those belong in plugin-tools). Keep commander, picocolors, @opentabs-dev/shared.

userStories.title: Add opentabs start command
userStories.description: As a user, I want an opentabs start command so that I can start the MCP server in production mode.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs start command exists with --port option
opentabs start spawns the MCP server as a child process (bun <server-entry>, no --hot, no --dev)
opentabs start tees output to terminal and ~/.opentabs/server.log (same pattern as current dev command)
opentabs start checks if port is already in use and shows a helpful error
opentabs start prints the MCP client config hint (how to add to ~/.claude/settings/mcp.json)
opentabs start forwards SIGINT/SIGTERM to the child process for graceful shutdown
opentabs start is the first command shown in opentabs --help
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Create platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts. This is similar to the current dev.ts but without --hot and without --dev. The key differences: (1) no bun --hot, (2) no --verbose or --log-level options (those are dev concerns), (3) the spawned process is just 'bun <server-entry>'. Resolve the server entry point the same way dev.ts does (import.meta.resolve @opentabs-dev/mcp-server with fallback). Register in cli.ts.

userStories.title: Update scaffold to use @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want the scaffolded project to depend on @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools so that my project has the right dev tooling.
acceptanceCriteria: Scaffolded package.json has @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools as devDependency instead of @opentabs-dev/cli
Scaffolded package.json scripts use opentabs-plugin instead of opentabs: build is 'tsc && opentabs-plugin build', dev is 'tsc --watch --preserveWatchOutput & opentabs-plugin build --watch'
scaffold.ts reads the plugin-tools version from the installed @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools package
create-opentabs-plugin package.json depends on @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools instead of @opentabs-dev/cli for scaffold
The scaffold export moves from @opentabs-dev/cli to @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools (or stays in cli since plugin create is a user command)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/cli/src/scaffold.ts to change the devDependency from @opentabs-dev/cli to @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools. Change the script references from 'opentabs build' to 'opentabs-plugin build' and 'opentabs build --watch' to 'opentabs-plugin build --watch'. The scaffold.ts file itself should stay in @opentabs-dev/cli since opentabs plugin create is a user command. The scaffold needs to read the plugin-tools version — it currently reads the cli version. Since cli depends on plugin-tools? No — keep it simple: the scaffold hardcodes the plugin-tools version from the cli's package.json or reads it from the installed node_modules. Actually, the scaffold currently reads the SDK version from import.meta.resolve('@opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk'). Add the same pattern for plugin-tools. Also update create-opentabs-plugin to depend on @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools instead of @opentabs-dev/cli for the scaffold export. Wait — actually the scaffold function itself lives in @opentabs-dev/cli. The create-opentabs-plugin imports it from @opentabs-dev/cli/scaffold. This should stay since opentabs plugin create is a user command and the scaffold logic belongs in the user CLI package. But create-opentabs-plugin's dependency should change to @opentabs-dev/cli (it already is). The only change in create-plugin is if the scaffold export path changes. Keep it in cli.

userStories.title: Update existing plugins to use opentabs-plugin command
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the existing plugins to use the new plugin-tools package so that they demonstrate the correct setup.
acceptanceCriteria: plugins/slack/package.json devDependencies has @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools instead of @opentabs-dev/cli
plugins/slack/package.json scripts use opentabs-plugin build instead of opentabs build
plugins/e2e-test/package.json devDependencies has @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools instead of @opentabs-dev/cli
plugins/e2e-test/package.json scripts use opentabs-plugin build instead of opentabs build
bun run build in plugins/slack succeeds after bun install
bun run build in plugins/e2e-test succeeds after bun install
userStories.notes: Edit plugins/slack/package.json and plugins/e2e-test/package.json. Change devDependency from @opentabs-dev/cli to @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools. Change build script from 'tsc && opentabs build' to 'tsc && opentabs-plugin build'. Change dev script from 'tsc --watch --preserveWatchOutput & opentabs build --watch' to 'tsc --watch --preserveWatchOutput & opentabs-plugin build --watch'. These are standalone projects with their own node_modules — they need bun install after the change. Since @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools is a new package not yet published to npm, this story may need to either: use a workspace link for development, or be done after publishing. For now, if they fail because plugin-tools isn't published yet, that's expected — the important thing is the package.json is correct.

userStories.title: Update publish script for new package
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the publish script to publish @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools so that plugin developers can install it.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/publish.sh bumps version in platform/plugin-tools/package.json
scripts/publish.sh updates cross-references: plugin-tools depends on shared and plugin-sdk
scripts/publish.sh publishes @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools in the correct dependency order (after shared and plugin-sdk, before or with cli)
Publish order is: shared → plugin-sdk → plugin-tools → cli
bun run build passes
userStories.notes: Edit scripts/publish.sh. Add platform/plugin-tools to the version bump loop. Add cross-reference updates for plugin-tools (depends on shared and plugin-sdk). Add the npm publish command for plugin-tools. The publish order must be: shared first (no deps), plugin-sdk (depends on shared), plugin-tools (depends on shared + plugin-sdk), cli last (depends on shared, may depend on plugin-tools for scaffold version). If cli doesn't depend on plugin-tools, then plugin-tools and cli can be published in any order after plugin-sdk.

userStories.title: Update CLI description and help text
userStories.description: As a user, I want the opentabs --help to clearly describe what the CLI does and show commands in a logical order.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs --help description is 'OpenTabs — manage your MCP server and plugins' (not 'develop, build, and manage plugins')
Commands are ordered logically: start, status, doctor, setup, logs, plugin, config
No developer-only commands appear in the help output
opentabs start is listed first as the primary command
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Edit platform/cli/src/cli.ts. Change the .description() string. Reorder the registerXxxCommand calls so start is first. Commander shows commands in registration order.

userStories.title: Update E2E test fixtures for CLI changes
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests to work with the new CLI structure so that tests pass.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E fixtures start the MCP server directly via bun (not via opentabs CLI) since tests control the server process
Any E2E tests that reference opentabs build are updated to reference opentabs-plugin build
Any E2E tests that reference opentabs dev or opentabs reload are updated or removed
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: Check e2e/fixtures.ts — the startMcpServer function likely spawns bun directly with the server entry point, not via the opentabs CLI. If so, no changes needed there. Search all E2E test files for references to 'opentabs build', 'opentabs dev', 'opentabs reload' and update as needed. The E2E tests mostly test the MCP server directly, not through the CLI.

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md for CLI separation
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want CLAUDE.md to document the two-CLI architecture so that AI assistants understand the separation.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md documents that @opentabs-dev/cli provides the opentabs binary (user-facing)
CLAUDE.md documents that @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools provides the opentabs-plugin binary (plugin developer)
CLAUDE.md lists the user CLI commands: start, status, doctor, setup, logs, plugin, config
CLAUDE.md lists the plugin-tools commands: build, build --watch
CLAUDE.md no longer references opentabs build, opentabs dev, or opentabs reload as user commands
The directory structure section includes platform/plugin-tools/
The publishing section includes @opentabs-dev/plugin-tools
userStories.notes: Edit CLAUDE.md. Update the architecture section to mention two CLIs. Update the directory structure to include platform/plugin-tools/. Update the commands section. Update the publishing section.

acceptanceCriteria: bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
opentabs --help shows only user commands: start, status, doctor, setup, logs, plugin, config
opentabs-plugin --help shows only: build

platform/plugin-tools/ has its own clean command set
